title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Browser and Node Client Systems · Compiler Case: Problem Details
summary: A support SDK receives 202, 204, redirects, problem details, opaque browser failures, and incompatible success payloads. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts problem details increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch typed response and failure classifier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse RFC 9457 problem details by type and extensions without scraping human-readable title or detail text. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
